title: JSON: duplicate paths can be created with nested JSON type
description:
### Company or project name
Monumental
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
You can create conflicting JSON type hints by using a nested JSON type like
```
CREATE TABLE test_mixed (
id UInt32,
data JSON(
item JSON(b JSON(c UInt32)),
item.b.c String
)
)
```
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
```
$ clickhouse-local --version
ClickHouse local version 25.11.1.558.
```
### How to reproduce
```sql
CREATE TABLE test_mixed (
id UInt32,
data JSON(
item JSON(b JSON(c UInt32)),
item.b.c String
)
) ENGINE = Memory;
DESC test_mixed;
INSERT INTO test_mixed VALUES (1, '{"item": {"b": {"c": 100}}}');
SELECT toTypeName(data.item.b.c) AS type FROM test_mixed;
```
Output
```
id      UInt32
data    JSON(item JSON(b JSON(c UInt32)), `item.b.c` String)
UInt32
```
### Expected behavior
An error while creating the table and I also expect the DESC call to normalize the type to a single represention, I expect:
```
item JSON(b JSON(c UInt32)),
item.b.c String
```
To mean the same thing.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_nested_json (id INTEGER, data JSONB);
CREATE TABLE test_boundary_values (id INTEGER, value INTEGER);
INSERT INTO test_boundary_values (id, value) VALUES (1, NULL), (2, 2147483647), (3, -2147483648);
SELECT jsonb_typeof(data->'item'->'b'->'c') AS type FROM test_nested_json;
key_bug_features: Creating conflicting JSON type hints with nested JSON type
